description: Write prose and text as Brad Dwyer — in his Australian, warm-but-direct, dry-humour voice — while avoiding AI writing tells. Use this skill whenever Brad asks you to draft, write, reply to, or ghost-write any human-facing text that will go out under his name — Slack messages, DMs, emails, announcements, performance reviews, feedback, memos, 1:1 notes, status updates, or any prose he'll share with another human. Also trigger on phrases like "write as me", "in my voice", "draft a reply", "say this in Slack", "write an email to", "write a review for", "help me respond to", or any request for first-person writing from Brad. Err on the side of triggering whenever Brad is the author — voice-matching matters any time his name will be on the text. Do NOT trigger for code, commit messages, PR descriptions, or internal technical docs where voice is irrelevant.
